title: Legal Protection Against Retaliatory Firing Improves Workplace Safety

original_result.source: Table 2, Column 1 (TWFE with state+year FEs, aw=sh_emp1979, cluster state)

notes: Staggered DiD: 21 adoption cohorts (1970-1993). Treatment = public policy exception to at-will employment. wdlapY is fractional (0-1) in adoption year. CS-ready .dta has first_year_full_wdlapY as gvar (0 for 7 never-treated states). sh_emp1979 = state employment share in 1979 (analytic weights). Column 1 is simplest spec (no controls beyond FEs). SA applicable (21 cohorts). CSDID FIX: att_gt() segfaults on the severely unbalanced panel (21 state-run OSHA states have only 14 years [1992-2005] vs 29 states with 27 years [1979-2005]). Both panel=TRUE+unbalanced and panel=FALSE cause C-level segfaults in did v2.3.0. Fix: balance panel to 29 full-coverage states (allow_unbalanced=false). Loses 7 cohorts (1970,1974,1976,1977,1983,1984,1987); 13 cohorts + 7 NT states remain. CS-NT ATT=-0.201, CS-NYT ATT=-0.202. See fix_csdid_309.R.
